title: The Roast

jokes.text: Did he just say 'merriment?' / I think he did. / Yes, I said 'merriment.' Because that's what happens when I spend too much time with my mother
jokes.setup: Patrick and Stevie react to David's formal language
jokes.punchline: David blames his mother for his pretentious vocabulary
jokes.explanation: Great character work showing David's self-awareness and the family dynamic influence

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: Why do you talk like that?

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Yeah, David. It's weird.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: My mother raised me to have a certain level of eloquence and refinement in my speech patterns.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: See? There it is again.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: It's not my fault. Blame Rose.

jokes.text: last week she told me to dance like an Indonesian scarf caught in the wind
jokes.setup: Moira giving dancing direction
jokes.punchline: Impossibly specific and pretentious dance instruction
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Moira absurdity - perfectly captures her over-the-top directing style

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Now, when you're moving through space, I want you to think of yourself as a delicate chrysalis unfurling in reverse. Not backwards, darling—that would be pedestrian. I want you to move as though you're a thought that Cocteau had and then immediately forgot.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: I'm not sure I understand what that means.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, of course you don't. That's precisely why you need my direction. Now, again—but this time, imagine you're a silk scarf caught in a gentle but artfully tragic wind.

jokes.text: I don't even know what that looks like
jokes.setup: The absurd Indonesian scarf direction
jokes.punchline: Patrick's matter-of-fact confusion
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response that grounds the absurdity

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: I'm sorry, which way is the scarf supposed to go?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: It's an Indonesian scarf, Patrick. It goes... Indonesian.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: Right, but what does that mean, exactly?

jokes.text: Yes, you have legs like tree trunks, we knew that going into this
jokes.setup: Stevie worried about being laughed off stage
jokes.punchline: David's brutal honesty delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Classic David - insulting but supportive, delivered with perfect timing

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, if it helps, I don't think anyone will laugh at you. I think they'll just sit in silence, which is somehow worse.

jokes.text: I made the mistake of going to Mario Lopez for advice, he was the hosting at the time
jokes.setup: David explaining why getting outside help is dangerous
jokes.punchline: Random, specific celebrity reference for pageant advice
jokes.explanation: Perfect blend of absurd specificity and character backstory

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You can't just bring in some random celebrity to give pageant advice. Do you know what happened the last time someone did that? Neither do I, because it's never happened, but I'm sure it was a disaster.

jokes.text: I've just been to Miami so many times before, and trust me, the 'wildlife' is not as sexy up close
jokes.setup: Ted mentions wildlife in Miami
jokes.punchline: Alexis thinks he means nightlife/party people
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis misunderstanding - reveals her party background

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: There's so much wildlife in Miami.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, yeah. The nightlife there is insane.

jokes.text: We just need to figure out where. / And what page is that? / Well, we both agree that we wanna get away, and we both agree that we're gonna land on somewhere so good
jokes.setup: Ted repeatedly suggests Galapagos, Alexis suggests other places
jokes.punchline: They're completely not on the same page while claiming they are
jokes.explanation: Good relationship comedy showing communication breakdown

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: So I was thinking we could go to the Galapagos Islands for our honeymoon.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my god, yes, totally. I was thinking more like... Paris? Or maybe the Maldives?

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Right, so Galapagos. We're on the same page.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Completely on the same page. I'm seeing Bora Bora, obviously.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Galapagos.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Mm-hmm, Bora Bora.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: We're so good at communicating.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: The best. I'll start looking at flights to... you know where.

jokes.text: [Awkward silence as Ted realizes Alexis hasn't understood his Galapagos hint at all]
jokes.setup: Ted has repeatedly tried to hint at the Galapagos, Alexis keeps suggesting other destinations
jokes.punchline: The silence reveals his realization that she's completely missed his point
jokes.explanation: Perfect use of silence to show Ted's dawning realization. The comedy is in what's not said - his internal frustration at the failed communication.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Alexis, I've been talking about the Galapagos for weeks now.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I know, and I think Cabo would be so much better for us.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: The Galapagos.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Or maybe Turks and Caicos?

jokes.text: Roland's extended fire metaphors about Moira's roasting ability
jokes.setup: Roland talking about missing Moira at the roast
jokes.punchline: The over-the-top fire metaphors that go on too long
jokes.explanation: Roland's tendency to overexplain jokes. The lack of economy actually serves his character.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: I miss Moira. She's like a fire that burns so bright, consuming everything in its path, leaving nothing but ash and devastation in her wake. A fire that spreads across the dry plains of my heart, engulfing every rational thought, every moment of peace, until I'm nothing but a smoldering heap of longing and regret. A fire that—

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Dad, she's been gone for three days.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: A FIRE, David!

jokes.text: Well, maybe you'd know, if you graduated elementary school
jokes.setup: Roland doesn't know what 'rippers' are
jokes.punchline: Johnny's attempted burn about Roland's education
jokes.explanation: Generic insult that sets up Johnny's overconfidence for later failure

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, Roland, that's because you didn't go to school. You were homeschooled by a man named Frank who lived in a van.

jokes.text: Look at your face! 'Oh Roland, I'm sorry, did I go too far?'
jokes.setup: Johnny's weak insult and immediate apology
jokes.punchline: Roland mocking Johnny's concern and mimicking his voice
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Johnny's overconfidence, shows the power dynamic

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Oh, I'm sorry, I didn't realize you were so sensitive. What are you gonna do, cry about it? Oh wait, you already are!

jokes.text: Oh pal, you are going to get eaten alive tonight!
jokes.setup: Johnny's failed practice insult
jokes.punchline: Roland's gleeful prediction of Johnny's doom
jokes.explanation: Sets up Johnny's later failure nicely, good character work

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Oh, you're gonna get eaten alive out there. You know that, right? You're gonna walk out there, and people are gonna see you, and they're gonna think, 'Who is this guy?' And then you're gonna open your mouth, and they're gonna know.

jokes.text: You make me sound like a feral cat
jokes.setup: Moira asking why David is walking alone
jokes.punchline: David's dramatic interpretation of her concern
jokes.explanation: Perfect David response - dramatic and self-deprecating

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Why are you walking alone?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Because apparently that's what I do now. I walk. Alone. Like some sort of... solitary figure in a Bergman film, pondering the meaninglessness of existence and my mother's inability to simply let me be.

jokes.text: a cheque that apparently is tax for the government, and not, as I assumed, a bonus for me
jokes.setup: David explaining why he's going home
jokes.punchline: His naive assumption about taxes being a bonus
jokes.explanation: Great character work showing David's business naivety

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm going home because I assumed taxes were a bonus.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Turns out they're not. They're actually money I owe.

jokes.text: you have been awfully clingy of late
jokes.setup: David wants to have dinner
jokes.punchline: Moira's blunt assessment
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira - brutally honest while using formal language

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: David wants to have dinner? That's wonderful. Does he know how to cook, or is he just going to stand there looking vaguely disappointed at a plate of food?

jokes.text: I think it might be propitious for you to spend some time with your peers
jokes.setup: Moira suggesting David needs other friends
jokes.punchline: Using 'propitious' in casual conversation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira vocabulary - overly formal for the situation

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: David, you really should cultivate other friendships. It's propitious to have a varied social circle.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Mom, no one uses 'propitious' in casual conversation.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, I just did, and it was quite propitious.

jokes.text: your show is stripping me of a social life
jokes.setup: David explaining why his friends are tired
jokes.punchline: Blaming Moira's demanding choreography
jokes.explanation: Good David dramatics, but relatively standard complaint humor

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: They're exhausted because my mother has them doing these incredibly elaborate dance numbers. She's been drilling them relentlessly.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: It's called *art*, David. And if my dancers can't handle a few simple choreographic sequences, then perhaps they shouldn't be in show business.

jokes.text: You employ the wrong font, and you may as well bid Auf Wiedersehen to ticket sales
jokes.setup: Discussing poster design
jokes.punchline: Dramatic warning about font choice with German phrase
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira over-dramatization with multilingual flourish

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: You cannot use that font. It's too pedestrian. It screams 'I have no taste and I shop at a strip mall.' Achtung, baby!

jokes.text: We're both going home though! What the hell?!
jokes.setup: Moira says goodbye but they're walking the same direction
jokes.punchline: David's confused reaction to the awkward goodbye
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment, relatable social confusion

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, goodbye!

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Wait, where are you going?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I'm heading this way.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: ...That's the way I'm going.

jokes.text: You mean, other than wanting to study some of the rarest and most diverse forms of plant and animal life on the planet?
jokes.setup: Alexis asking why anyone would go to Galapagos
jokes.punchline: Ted's obvious scientific explanation delivered sarcastically
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - Ted's passion vs Alexis's disinterest

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Well, actually, the Galapagos Islands are a UNESCO World Heritage Site with unique endemic species found nowhere else on Earth. They're a living laboratory of evolution, which is why Charles Darwin himself conducted his most important research there in 1835. So I would say that's a pretty good reason to go.

jokes.text: You know, Emmett, the tortoise is 180-years-old, So I'm sure he'll still be around in another few years
jokes.setup: Ted trying to downplay the importance of the opportunity
jokes.punchline: Using tortoise longevity to justify waiting
jokes.explanation: Sweet and awkward - Ted trying to convince himself

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Look, I know it seems like a big opportunity, but I'm not going to rush into anything. Tortoises live for over 100 years, and they seem pretty happy about it. So what's the rush?

jokes.text: once-in-a-lifetime job opportunity
jokes.setup: Ted calling the opportunity 'dumb'
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting himself with its actual importance
jokes.explanation: Good internal conflict moment, realistic relationship dynamic

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: That's a dumb opportunity.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Actually, no, wait—this could change everything for the business.

jokes.text: I once maintained a successful, semi-committed text relationship with Josh Hartnett while he was shooting 'Pearl Harbor'
jokes.setup: Discussing long-distance relationships
jokes.punchline: Alexis's absurd celebrity connection story
jokes.explanation: Classic Alexis backstory - specific, absurd, and perfectly delivered

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, I was in a long-distance relationship once. With this guy who was a stunt double for a stunt double on a Netflix series that got cancelled after two episodes.

jokes.text: That was a good talk for everyone
jokes.setup: Tense conversation about Ted's sacrifice
jokes.punchline: Alexis's awkward attempt to end uncomfortable topic
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Alexis's discomfort with serious topics

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: So... Ted's really giving up his whole career for me?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Yes, Alexis. He's moving across the country.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God. Well... that's... I mean, should I do something nice for him?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I don't know, maybe tell him you love him?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Right, yes. Okay. Um, anyway, so I was thinking about getting a blowout tomorrow—

jokes.text: Babe, do you have a dishwasher? / Okay, I'm just gonna... I'm gonna figure it out then
jokes.setup: Alexis deciding to do dishes as gesture
jokes.punchline: Doesn't know if Ted has basic appliances, tries anyway
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Alexis's sheltered upbringing

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I'm going to do the dishes. It's a gesture.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I mean, I don't know if Ted even has a dishwasher. Or a sink. Or running water, for that matter.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: But I'm going to try anyway.

jokes.text: Sorry, you know those days where you're just like, super in love? / Hmm, not really
jokes.setup: Alexis asking about being in love
jokes.punchline: Twyla's honest negative response
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Alexis's privilege and Twyla's reality

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Have you ever been in love?

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: No, not really. I mean, I thought I was in love with Ted once, but then he started talking about soil pH levels and I realized I was just lonely.

jokes.text: Like, he gives me so much, and I have learned to take as well
jokes.setup: Discussing give and take in relationships
jokes.punchline: Alexis's version of 'give and take' is just taking
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character work - Alexis thinks she's evolved but reveals her selfishness

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Well, I think in a relationship, it's all about give and take. You know, you give a little, you take a little.

jokes.text: Someone who would literally put all their hopes and dreams aside to be with me
jokes.setup: Twyla commenting on Ted's sacrifice
jokes.punchline: Highlighting how selfish this actually sounds
jokes.explanation: Twyla's subtle way of making Alexis think about the situation

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: Oh, Ted's so selfless. He's staying in Schitt's Creek just for me.

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: Wait... he's staying in Schitt's Creek? For me?

jokes.text: You're like my little fairy godmother, if she wore an apron, and Celine Dion's perfume
jokes.setup: Alexis thanking Twyla for advice
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific comparison including Canadian pop star
jokes.explanation: Classic Alexis - sweet sentiment delivered with bizarre specificity

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Thank you so much for the advice, Twyla. You know, you're like the Canadian version of Oprah, but less famous and with better skin.

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: Oh my gosh, thank you!

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I was talking about the skin thing.

jokes.text: It's a bit too European for my taste
jokes.setup: Johnny asking for feedback on his material
jokes.punchline: Bob criticizing his aftershave instead
jokes.explanation: Good Bob moment - missing the point entirely

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: So what did you think of my material?

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Well, I think you're wearing too much aftershave.

jokes.text: The phone book? How old are these?!
jokes.setup: Johnny starting a joke about phone books
jokes.punchline: Ronnie pointing out how dated the reference is
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about outdated comedy material

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: You know, phone books used to be so thick, you couldn't even lift them with one hand.

dialogue.speaker: Ronnie
dialogue.line: Yeah, because they don't exist anymore, you old fossil.

jokes.text: Johnny, you need a search and rescue team to find the punchlines for those setups
jokes.setup: Johnny's long, unfunny setups
jokes.punchline: Ronnie's cutting metaphor about lost punchlines
jokes.explanation: Excellent roast joke with perfect metaphor - exactly what the show is about

dialogue.speaker: Ronnie
dialogue.line: It's like watching someone throw a dart at a board that isn't there.

jokes.text: Just stay away from spouses, kids, and health, and you'll be fine. Like Roland in the bedroom
jokes.setup: Ronnie giving comedy advice
jokes.punchline: Immediate example roasting Roland's performance
jokes.explanation: Classic roast humor - crude but effective demonstration

dialogue.speaker: Ronnie
dialogue.line: You gotta commit to the bit. Don't half-ass it.

dialogue.speaker: Ronnie
dialogue.line: Like, look at Roland over there doing his little song and dance. That's half-assing it right there.

jokes.text: It's 7:15. Are you sure you should be walking over there alone at this hour?
jokes.setup: David trying to prevent Moira from going to rehearsal
jokes.punchline: Treating 7:15 PM like dangerous late night
jokes.explanation: Good David dramatics - making a normal evening sound dangerous

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Mom, you can't go out at 7:15 PM. That's basically the middle of the night.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: David, it's 7:15 in the evening.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Exactly. That's dangerous late night hours.

jokes.text: What the hell?! / I don't-I don't know
jokes.setup: David and Moira discovering the secret rehearsal
jokes.punchline: Their shocked reactions to the elaborate performance
jokes.explanation: Good payoff moment for the deception storyline

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: What is... what is happening right now?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I have no idea, but the production value is surprisingly high.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Is that a spotlight? Where did they get a spotlight?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: And the costumes! David, look at the costumes!

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm looking. I'm looking, and I hate it.

jokes.text: skullduggery, when it leads to such thrilling results, a pinch of moral corruption is a small price to pay
jokes.setup: Moira discovering the deception
jokes.punchline: Forgiving it because the result impressed her
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira - dramatic language forgiving deception for artistic results

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: You deceived me. You lied to my face.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I did, and I'm sorry.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: However... the result is rather impressive. I suppose I can forgive it.

jokes.text: I loosened the pickle jar. Derek merely popped the lid
jokes.setup: David asking if Moira choreographed the number
jokes.punchline: Moira's colorful metaphor for collaboration
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Moira metaphor - perfectly captures her need to take credit while being gracious

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: David, collaboration is like a beautiful tapestry. You can't just pull one thread without the whole thing unraveling. It's a delicate dance of give and take, a symphony of souls intertwined in the pursuit of artistic excellence.

jokes.text: Yes, Ted Mullens, I will go to the Cayman Islands with you. / Uh, you mean the Galapagos?
jokes.setup: Alexis making the big decision to go
jokes.punchline: Still getting the destination wrong
jokes.explanation: Good callback - shows Alexis still isn't really listening

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I'm finally ready to make the big decision and go.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: That's great! Where are you thinking?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Well, I've narrowed it down to two places: New York or Los Angeles.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Those are both great cities. Which one are you leaning towards?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I'm thinking... Switzerland.

jokes.text: Yes! Let's celebrate! / Uh, no, no, no, I meant like, vaccinations
jokes.setup: Ted mentioning they need shots
jokes.punchline: Alexis thinks party drinks, Ted means medical shots
jokes.explanation: Perfect miscommunication joke showing different priorities

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: We really need to get some shots.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my god, yes! I love shots. What are we thinking, tequila?

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: I was actually referring to vaccinations.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh.

jokes.text: And boy, does he have a lot of gut to go with
jokes.setup: Roland likes to go with his gut when making decisions
jokes.punchline: Johnny pointing out Roland's large stomach
jokes.explanation: Basic fat joke - functional but not creative

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, Roland, you certainly have a lot of gut to go with.

jokes.text: And look at Bob, pretending to get it. And we all know Gwen, he hasn't gotten it in years!
jokes.setup: Bob pretending to understand something
jokes.punchline: Double meaning about understanding vs sexual satisfaction
jokes.explanation: Standard double entendre, nothing special

jokes.text: Johnny's 'rippers' and 'starched sleeve' banter
jokes.setup: Johnny trying to sound confident about his roast material
jokes.punchline: His awkward use of terminology and Roland's comeback about starched sleeves
jokes.explanation: Shows Johnny trying to fit in with roast culture while Roland effortlessly outplays him.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I've been workshopping some real zingers. Very cutting edge material.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Cutting edge? Johnny, the only thing sharp about you is the starch in your sleeves.

jokes.text: a few tasty little rippers that are more palatable than anything Jocelyn's been serving up tonight
jokes.setup: Johnny starting his roast set
jokes.punchline: Using 'rippers' again and roasting the food
jokes.explanation: Callback to the rippers joke, basic food roast

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: You know, I've been thinking about the food here. These 'rippers' — that's what we call them — they're absolutely devastating. Not in a good way. I ordered something that was supposed to be chicken. I'm still not entirely sure what it was. It had the texture of a hockey puck and the flavor profile of regret. My wife actually said it was the worst thing she's ever put in her mouth, and she's been married to me for thirty years.

jokes.text: we all know if you've ever taken your car to Bob's Garage, he's not necessarily known 'four helpings' anyone
jokes.setup: Bob joking about Johnny having four helpings
jokes.punchline: Johnny's confused attempt at a comeback about Bob's service
jokes.explanation: Weak, confusing wordplay that doesn't quite work - shows Johnny struggling

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, maybe if your service was better, I wouldn't need four helpings.

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: My service? You've been coming to this restaurant for years.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Yes, and I'm still waiting for the appetizer.

jokes.text: Which is what the town must've been thinking when Roland elected himself Mayor
jokes.setup: Things going downhill fast
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to Roland becoming mayor
jokes.explanation: Johnny's first successful joke - good structure and local reference

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Things going downhill fast? That's like comparing it to Roland becoming mayor.

jokes.text: Johnny's Roland Mayor election joke
jokes.setup: Things going downhill fast
jokes.punchline: Like when Roland elected himself Mayor
jokes.explanation: Johnny's first genuinely successful joke that gets a good reaction and shows improvement.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Like when Roland elected himself Mayor

jokes.text: The last time anyone saw her this happy was at a 3-for-1 sale on cargo pants!
jokes.setup: Ronnie smiling at Johnny's previous joke
jokes.punchline: Specific joke about Ronnie's fashion preferences
jokes.explanation: Great character-specific roast - perfectly captures Ronnie's practical style

jokes.text: which is what a car is supposed to do... unless you take it to Bob's Garage!
jokes.setup: Johnny trying to explain his car-go pun
jokes.punchline: Repeating the Bob's garage reference
jokes.explanation: Johnny over-explaining and repeating - classic comedy mistake

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: It's a car-go. You know, like cargo, but it's a car. Car-go.

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Yeah, I got it.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: At Bob's garage. Car-go to Bob's garage.

jokes.text: Which reminds me of the last time I was in Roland's bedroom, um... well, I-I wasn't in Roland's bedroom
jokes.setup: Johnny setting up a bedroom joke
jokes.punchline: Immediately backing out when he realizes what he's saying
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Johnny's discomfort with dirty humor

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I was just thinking, you know, a good bedroom setup is really important. The right lighting, the right—

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: —no, no, I'm not doing this.

jokes.text: with all the venom of a silkworm
jokes.setup: Johnny claiming he killed them with comedy
jokes.punchline: Moira's cutting metaphor about his lack of bite
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Moira metaphor - original, cutting, and perfectly delivered

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: You didn't kill them with comedy. You suffocated them with the pillow of your personality.

jokes.text: like you did with your education
jokes.setup: Alexis saying to take it easy
jokes.punchline: Johnny roasting his own daughter's education
jokes.explanation: Dark family humor - Johnny getting into the roast spirit

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Take it easy? Alexis, you went to a school where the mascot was a participation trophy.

jokes.text: not smart enough to stop wearing sweaters in the middle of summer
jokes.setup: David being smart enough to get the joke
jokes.punchline: But not smart enough about his fashion choices
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific observation roast

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: David being smart enough to get the joke, but not smart enough about his fashion choices.

jokes.text: Something the good people at Blockbuster never said about Rose Video
jokes.setup: Johnny being called a worthy competitor
jokes.punchline: Referencing his business failure
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to their business history - specific and cutting

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, of course he's a worthy competitor. He's competed in every business venture he's ever attempted... and lost spectacularly in each one.

jokes.text: That was low, like David's standards
jokes.setup: Moira's Blockbuster roast
jokes.punchline: Johnny hitting back at David
jokes.explanation: Generic standards joke but good escalation

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, David, at least I didn't name my business after myself. Oh wait, I did. But the difference is, people actually wanted to rent from Blockbuster.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: That's rich coming from a man who opened a store in a town where people still use VCRs.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: And it's still more successful than whatever you're doing over there.

jokes.text: Or the placement of Alexis' back tattoo
jokes.setup: Talking about things being low
jokes.punchline: Revealing Alexis has a lower back tattoo
jokes.explanation: Good revelation joke that adds character backstory

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, I think we've all hit a low point.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I have a lower back tattoo.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Yes, that's what I was referring to.

jokes.text: It says 'that's hot' in Cantonese. Got it in Hong Kong. / I was 12-years-old!
jokes.setup: The revelation about the tattoo
jokes.punchline: The absurd details and young age when she got it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis backstory - specific, absurd, and reveals her wild past

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I got it when I was like, seven.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Seven?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Yeah, at this place in Cabo. The guy didn't really ask a lot of questions.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: That's illegal.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I know that now, Mother.

tmdb_overview: Johnny fills in for Moira at the annual Mayor’s Roast, as David distracts Moira from finding out that Stevie and Patrick have been taking private dance lessons.
insight: Rose family roasts themselves at 2.11 jokes-per-minute, breaking their deadpan comfort zone.
